topic: China variable pay and bonus payroll controls for foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Anchors contract and wage-related rights framework relevant to variable compensation handling.
Supports documentation and lawful-treatment expectations in dispute scenarios.

sources.title: Labor Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Provides wage payment and labor-protection baseline principles relevant to payroll execution.
Supports employer duty to administer compensation in compliant and documented manner.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Provides payroll tax context for salary/bonus treatment and annual reconciliation environment.
Supports need for pre-run classification of payment components and tax logic documentation.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Reinforces that multinational employer risk is often process and documentation quality.
Supports operations-first compliance posture for payroll-sensitive employment decisions.

consensus: Variable pay risk is primarily caused by weak approvals, unclear eligibility records, and late payroll conversion changes.
Foreign employers need a controlled workflow from compensation decision to payroll release.
Evidence quality and communication alignment are essential for defensibility.
uncertainties: City-level practice and case-specific interpretation can differ in dispute handling.
Specific tax-treatment details may require local confirmation before final run.
English-language sources may not include all implementing nuances in Chinese-language materials.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: Use a D-10 to D+5 variable-pay payroll runbook with approval lock, component-level tax notes, and post-run reconciliation evidence.
